PL-PBF validation — figures

2026-07-17 · kernel acceptance + injection smokes (nlive 40–60; authoritative nlive=250 grid queued behind the production mass-refit) · companion to the review brief

1 · Kernel acceptance: Cordes Fig 40 (left) vs our implementation (right)

All three regimes reproduced: shared exponential core to the crossover, every curve riding the t−β/2 power law (dotted) until it peels off at its own inner-scale cutoff, ordered by si. Nesting is exact to machine zero: si→∞ equals the production power-law PBF, si≤sc (and β→4) equal the pure exponential — our current EMG. An earlier cutoff-placement bug (anchored at the crossover instead of si) was caught by injection recovery and fixed; recovery is now unbiased (β 3.5025 vs 3.5, τ exact, si identifiable).

2 · The headline: the wrong-PBF α artifact depends on bandwidth lever arm

Left: fitting our current EMG to a heavy-tailed injection with true α=4.67 — the joint CHIME+DSA fit and CHIME-only fit recover α to −0.06; the DSA-only fit (15% fractional bandwidth) collapses below 4 with wide errors, exactly the Cordes Fig-58 artifact. Sub-4 scattering indices from narrow-band single-instrument fits are suspect as a class; our joint methodology is immune. Right: the joint-fit α bias from EMG mismatch is bounded (≈−0.03 as S/N→∞) and shrinks with si because the far tail sits below the noise floor — the EMG-vs-PL discrimination lives in the near tail (t/τe ~ 3–15). Consequence: the PL-PBF change moves residual structure and evidence, not the headline α or TOAs — the β>3.98 limit is robust to PBF-shape systematics.

3 · The rail, visualized: before/after RFI fix (production samples)

Why the production “α=4.00” is a limit, not a measurement: α is a clamped spike (definitional for β≥3.98), β is a uniform pile-up against its prior ceiling, while τ1GHz is a genuine, well-measured posterior. Red (old binning) and blue (RFI-fixed) overlay almost exactly — the mask-aware binning fix does not move the screen parameters on either burst. The unclamped α constraint comes from the relaxed-α A/B now queued (jobs 70/71).

4 · Component vetting behind the corners above (owner-requested)

Band-summed data vs model with normalized residuals for the fits behind Section 3, checking that no burst component was left unfit. casey: single peak in both bands; the residual is the documented ±dipole at the peak (30.6σ C / 9.1σ D) — shape error, not a missing component. wilhelm D: single clean peak with the known single-bin dipole (26.7σ). wilhelm C is the one flagged ambiguity: the model undershoots the peak (+7.6σ narrow run) and overshoots the decay (−3…−5σ broad run). The evidence ladder rejected a second component (C2) and attributed this to shape mismatch; visually, an unfit narrow component riding a broad base remains an alternative reading. The two readings are discriminated by the PL-PBF test: sharp peak + fast early decay + long late tail is precisely the heavy-tailed-PBF signature, so wilhelm is a primary real-data test case for the model change.
